creator: jwb@freebsd.org
text:
(In reply to sigsys from comment #5)

You ain't kidding...

I had a 16G vmem limit set on my 8G RAM laptop just to prevent low-quality bioinformatics software from overwhelming my swap.

When I remove the limit, chromium works and this is ps shows for a single chromium window:

 UID   PID  PPID C PRI NI      VSZ    RSS MWCHAN   STAT TT         TIME COMMAND
2000 24760 24625 1  27  0   376072 262396 select   S+    0      0:01.04 chrome:
2000 24762 24760 1  23  0   364852 265332 select   S+    0      0:00.38 chrome:
2000 24763 24760 1  42  0   283728 226744 uwait    S+    0      0:00.15 chrome:
2000 24764 24760 0  42  0   279444 224956 uwait    S+    0      0:00.12 chrome:
2000 24765 24760 3  31 10 25481000 240272 uwait    SN+   0      0:00.32 chrome:
2000 24766 24760 1  32 10 25484460 254412 uwait    SN+   0      0:00.80 chrome:
2000 24768 24760 3  45 10 25471652 229740 uwait    SN+   0      0:00.12 chrome:

That's 3 processes using 24G virtual each + 4 more reasonable ones.

Not a fan of the "address space is free" mentality...

Thanks for the lead.
